summary: Comprehensive backend + frontend testing of CookPilot MVP (Phase 1). Backend: 17/17 pytest tests passing covering health, auth (login/me/missing-token/invalid), recipes CRUD + favorite toggle, shopping flow + from-recipe + from-low-stock + clear-checked, pantry CRUD + adjust + low-stock, invites + accept-invite, users admin-only + non-admin 403, settings (public fields only, no secret leak), widgets (catalog, dashboard, tablet, save), Aria SSO + allergies + purchases aggregate + secret enforcement, chat 503 when OpenAI not configured, receipts upload, purchases. Frontend: login/logout, dashboard render, recipes page, shopping add/clear, pantry add, chat gracefully handles missing API key with German toast + inline message, tablet page loads, admin 7-tab layout with invites (invite link created), OpenAI key input, widgets save toast working.
critical_code_review_comments: Backend is well-structured into small router files (<200 lines each). Models clean, ConfigDict(extra='ignore') consistently used, _id excluded via projection everywhere.
Settings public endpoint properly hides secrets and returns *_set booleans - good security practice.
Aria endpoints correctly enforce shared_secret and return 503 when not configured vs 401 when wrong secret - clean separation.
Chat endpoint raises LLMNotConfigured -> 503 with explicit German message; frontend surfaces it via inline warning + toast.
Minor: receipts_router OCR is hooked but pytesseract/Tesseract binary not installed in preview env - by design (Phase 3 prep). Upload-only flow works as expected.
Minor: /api/shopping/from-recipe accepts recipe_id; consider also documenting a servings override in OpenAPI examples.
Minor: Password policy not enforced on accept-invite (accepted 'Passwort!2026' OK, but no min-length validation visible in AcceptInviteRequest model).
seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_-prefixed recipes, shopping items, pantry items, invites, users via accept-invite, purchases, and an Aria SSO user. Some test data intentionally left in DB (invites, users from accept-invite) since there's no admin delete-user API tested here and those are normal MVP records.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test suite at /app/backend/tests/test_cookpilot_api.py is reusable. Run: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_cookpilot_api.py -v. ARIA shared secret is set to 'test-secret' after the settings tests ran; OpenAI key is intentionally empty. Admin seeded account is admin@cookpilot.local. Frontend routes all work with data-testids: login-email-input, login-password-input, login-submit-btn, new-recipe-btn, shopping-{name|amount|unit}-input, shopping-add-btn, shopping-clear-checked-btn, pantry-{name|amount|unit|min}-input, pantry-add-btn, chat-input, chat-send-btn, admin-tab-{users|invites|ai|smtp|aria|widgets-dashboard|widgets-tablet}, invite-email-input, invite-create-btn, admin-openai-key-input, widgets-save-dashboard/tablet.
rca of the issue: No bugs found. Initial test failure was only a test-side issue (backend lowercases email on invite creation, as expected) - fixed in test by case-insensitive comparison.
